verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase that is predominantly inner-leaflet anchored at the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, placing its kinase domain on the cytoplasmic face — not the extracellular face. However, SRC has well-documented cancer-cell ecto-kinase biology: tumor cells externalize SRC (and other Src-family kinases) to the outer leaflet, where it is accessible to extracellular antibodies and can be targeted therapeutically. This cancer-state ecto-display qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
